title: Bitsgap Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: Bitsgap Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Bitsgap Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Bitsgap (4.2★, $29/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Bitsgap — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Bitsgap?

Bitsgap is a cloud-based crypto trading bot platform founded in 2017 and headquartered in Tallinn, Estonia, that automates trading across 25+ cryptocurrency exchanges through a unified dashboard. The platform specializes in multiple bot types—Grid, DCA (Dollar-Cost Averaging), BTD (Buy the Dip), COMBO, and LOOP bots—allowing traders to execute passive income strategies without manually executing trades. With a solid 4.2/5 rating, Bitsgap serves crypto traders who want to automate grid and DCA strategies, manage multi-exchange portfolios, and test strategies before risking real capital. Starting at $29/month for the Basic tier, it's designed for traders ready to move beyond manual trading but who want to keep their funds non-custodial on their own exchange accounts.

How to Create Your Bitsgap Account

Creating your Bitsgap account takes about 5–10 minutes and requires minimal information upfront:

  • Sign up: Go to Bitsgap's website and click "Create Account" or "Sign Up." You'll need a valid email address and a strong password (Bitsgap requires passwords with uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols).
  • Verify your email: Check your inbox for a verification link from Bitsgap. Click it to confirm your email address. This step is mandatory before you can access the platform.
  • Add basic profile information: You'll optionally fill in your name, country, and preferred currency for dashboard display. This isn't strictly required to start using the platform, but filling it out improves your experience.
  • Enable two-factor authentication (2FA): Bitsgap strongly recommends enabling 2FA immediately after signup. Use an authenticator app like Google Authenticator or Authy rather than SMS for stronger security. Store your backup codes in a safe location.
  • Accept terms and begin your free trial: Agree to Bitsgap's terms of service to activate your 7-day free trial with full Pro-tier access. No credit card is required to start the trial, which is a major advantage over competitors like 3Commas.

The entire process is straightforward with no verification documents needed at signup (KYC verification only becomes relevant if you later use Bitsgap's conversion features for fiat transactions, which most traders skip).

Setting Up Bitsgap for the First Time

Once logged in, you'll land on the main dashboard. Here's what to configure before deploying your first bot:

1. Connect your exchange account: Navigate to Settings > API Keys and select your exchange from the 25+ supported platforms (Binance, Coinbase, Kraken, Bybit, Kucoin, etc.). Generate an API key on your exchange with trading permissions enabled but withdrawal disabled for safety. Paste the API key and secret into Bitsgap—the connection is non-custodial, meaning Bitsgap never touches your funds. This step is critical and takes 5 minutes per exchange.

2. Explore the portfolio dashboard: The main dashboard shows your total balance, equity distribution across holdings, and real-time performance metrics. If you connected multiple exchanges, they all aggregate here, giving you a true view of your multi-exchange portfolio. This unified view is one of Bitsgap's strongest advantages over single-exchange bots.

3. Set your trading preferences: Go to Settings > Preferences and configure your default leverage (if using futures), base currency for reporting, and timezone. If you plan to use COMBO bots (which support futures), enable futures mode here.

4. Review notification settings: Bitsgap sends alerts when bots complete, hit stop-loss, or encounter errors. Configure these in Settings > Notifications. Most traders enable email and mobile app notifications to catch issues immediately rather than checking the dashboard constantly.

5. Familiarize yourself with the bot builder: Click "Create Bot" to explore the interface without deploying anything. You'll see dropdowns for bot type, exchange, trading pair, and parameters. During your free trial, test-run at least one paper trading bot to understand the workflow before committing real capital.

Essential Features You Should Know

Grid Bots: Grid bots automate buying low and selling high within a price range. Set your price range, grid count, and investment amount, and the bot places buy orders at intervals below current price and sell orders above. Ideal for sideways markets, grid bots execute dozens of micro-profits without needing you to time the market. Grid trading on Bitsgap works across all 25+ exchanges, making it more accessible than exchange-native grids that vary by platform.

DCA Bots (Dollar-Cost Averaging): DCA bots invest a fixed amount at regular intervals (hourly, daily, weekly) regardless of price, reducing the impact of volatility. Set your investment amount and interval, and the bot automatically purchases. This removes emotion from investing and is perfect for building long-term positions in volatile assets. Bitsgap's DCA implementation includes a "Safety Order" feature that adds extra buys on price dips, amplifying gains in uptrends.

COMBO Bots: COMBO bots combine long and short futures positions with a single entry signal—essential for traders betting on volatility or hedging. Deploy a long and short simultaneously with synchronized stop-losses and take-profits. This feature sets Bitsgap apart from grid-only bots and appeals to traders with derivatives experience.

Paper Trading: Test any bot configuration with simulated trades using real price data before risking actual capital. Paper trading runs your bot logic against live order books but doesn't execute real orders. This is invaluable for validating your strategy. Your 7-day free trial includes full paper trading access—use it extensively.

Performance Analytics: Every completed bot displays charts showing entry/exit prices, profit/loss, win rate, and ROI. Bitsgap tracks all historical trades in a searchable log. This data is critical for understanding what works and optimizing future bots. Unlike Cryptohopper, Bitsgap doesn't lock analytics behind premium tiers.

Smart Orders: Bitsgap's Smart Orders extend your exchange's native capabilities by adding stop-loss, take-profit, and trailing stops to market orders that your exchange doesn't natively support. This bridges the gap between basic and advanced exchange features, particularly useful on exchanges like Binance US with limited order types.

Mobile App: iOS and Android apps let you manage bots, view portfolios, and receive alerts on the go. The mobile interface mirrors the web dashboard, so you can deploy or pause bots from anywhere. Push notifications ensure you never miss critical alerts about your trading activity.

Bitsgap Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Basic Plan — $29/month: Supports 1 active Grid bot and 1 active DCA bot simultaneously. Limited to spot trading (no futures). Includes paper trading and basic analytics. Best for beginners testing strategies with small capital before upgrading. No credit card needed for the 7-day trial.

Advanced Plan — $69/month: Scales to 10 active bots across Grid, DCA, and BTD types. Adds COMBO bot support (futures pairs) and higher API request limits for more frequent bot checks. This is the sweet spot for intermediate traders running 3–5 simultaneous strategies. The jump from Basic to Advanced is where you unlock meaningful multi-strategy capability.

Pro Plan — $149/month: Unlimited active bots, LOOP bot access (perpetual low-frequency trading), priority support, and advanced backtesting across longer timeframes. For professional traders or teams running 10+ simultaneous strategies, the unlimited bot count justifies the cost. However, at $149/month, the Pro tier is expensive compared to Cryptohopper's $99 equivalent—you're paying a premium for non-custody and the specific bot types Bitsgap specializes in.

Recommendation by trader type: Beginners should use the 7-day free Pro trial to test all bot types, then drop to Basic ($29/month) to keep costs minimal while learning. Intermediate traders operating 3–5 strategies should start with Advanced ($69/month) for COMBO bot access. Advanced traders with diversified multi-strategy operations should go Pro for unlimited bots, though many experienced traders stay on Advanced and manage bots in batches.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Bitsgap

1. Use paper trading to validate every strategy before deployment: Don't deploy a grid bot with real capital until you've run it in paper trading for at least 48 hours. Bitsgap's paper trading uses live order books but doesn't execute real trades, so you can see exactly how your bot would perform without risk. This single habit prevents costly configuration mistakes.

2. Set realistic grid ranges based on technical support/resistance: The most common grid bot mistake is setting the price range too wide, which traps capital in dead zones. Use Bitsgap's integrated charting or external tools like TradingView to identify support and resistance levels, then set your grid to capture 3–5% moves within that zone. Narrow, focused grids outperform wide ones because capital stays active.

3. Automate DCA with safety orders during downtrends: Enable Bitsgap's Safety Order feature on DCA bots to add extra buys when price drops 2–5% from your last purchase. This amplifies gains when markets recover but doesn't trigger endlessly in long crashes. Combine this with a hard stop-loss to cap losses if the trend continues down.

4. Monitor bot logs weekly and disable underperformers: Bitsgap tracks every bot action in a detailed log accessible from your dashboard. Set a weekly 15-minute review: check which bots hit their take-profit targets consistently and which underperform, then disable losers. This active management prevents capital from sitting in stalled bots.

5. Use multiple smaller bots instead of one large bot: Instead of deploying one $5,000 grid bot, deploy five $1,000 grid bots on different pairs. This diversifies your risk—if one market crashes, the others aren't impacted. Bitsgap's Advanced and Pro plans support this multi-bot approach, whereas Basic is too limited for this strategy.

6. Stagger bot start times across different hours to average entry: If deploying multiple grid bots on the same pair (which Bitsgap allows), start them at different times so they establish positions at different price points. This naturally averages your entry cost across time and reduces slippage from mass execution.

7. Test COMBO bots extensively in paper mode before futures trading: COMBO bots are powerful but complex—they open simultaneous long and short positions with synchronized stops. Paper-test for several days to understand how they behave in volatile markets before committing real capital to futures trading.

Common Bitsgap Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "Exchange API key invalid" error after connecting: This usually means your exchange didn't generate the key correctly or you copied it with extra spaces. Solution: Delete the key from Bitsgap's settings, go back to your exchange and regenerate it, then paste carefully without trailing spaces. Verify that "Trading" permissions are enabled on the key in your exchange settings. Binance users should also check that IP whitelisting isn't restricting Bitsgap's servers.

Issue 2: Grid bot completes but shows no profit: Grid bots need volatility to profit—if price stays flat, there are no buy/sell cycles. Solution: Check your grid range; if it's too wide relative to the asset's typical daily swing, the bot won't trigger enough cycles. Narrow your range to capture 2–3% swings, or switch to a DCA bot if you're holding a position long-term.

Issue 3: Paper trading shows profit but real bot loses money: This happens when slippage eats into thin margins. Paper trading assumes your orders fill at mid-price, but real exchange orders may fill worse, especially on low-liquidity pairs. Solution: Only run grid bots on high-liquidity pairs (top 20 by volume on your exchange), increase your grid count to reduce spread impact, or accept slightly larger grid ranges to accommodate real-world slippage.

Issue 4: High API request warnings or bot delays: If Bitsgap warns about API rate limits, you're checking bot status too frequently or running too many bots on the Basic plan. Solution: Upgrade to Advanced or Pro (higher API limits), reduce the number of active bots, or increase bot check intervals if your exchange allows.

Is Bitsgap Worth It? Our Verdict

Bitsgap justifies its $29–$149 price range for traders serious about multi-exchange automation, particularly those deploying Grid and DCA strategies. The non-custodial model, 25+ exchange support, and 7-day free Pro trial are significant advantages—you can validate the platform at full capability before paying a cent. The 4.2/5 rating reflects reliable execution, though the higher price tier ($149/month for Pro) positions it above competitors like Cryptohopper for traders who don't need unlimited bots. Best for intermediate and advanced crypto traders automating passive income strategies across multiple exchanges; beginners should test the free trial and consider Basic ($29/month) before committing. Skip Bitsgap if you trade traditional assets (stocks, forex, commodities)—it's crypto-only—or if you need custom API scripting, which Bitsgap doesn't offer natively. See our full Bitsgap review for detailed comparisons with Bitsgap vs. Cryptohopper and other alternatives.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
